headline: Immutable core contracts with token-governed fee/internal parameters

findings.text: PoolManager (0x0000...8A90) is owned by the Uniswap Timelock (0x1a9C...35BC). Core logic is immutable; the owner role is primarily for protocol fee management.
Core contracts (PoolManager, PositionManager) are verified direct implementations, not proxies. Bytecode is immutable; T1 upgradeability is unreachable.
The uncontested fast path delay is ~10 days: 13,140 blocks (~2d) votingDelay + 40,320 blocks (~6d) votingPeriod + 172,800s (2d) timelock delay.
Governance uses GovernorBravo (0x408E...24C3) with token-weighted voting. Quorum is 40,000,000 UNI (4% of total supply).
Highest reachable tier is T2/T3: governance can change protocol fee parameters (T2) and internal governance rules (T3). User-fund-holding logic is immutable (T1 unreachable).

steelman.red: Governance could theoretically redirect protocol fees to an attacker, though this does not impact user principal in pools.
steelman.orange: The 2-day timelock delay is relatively short for a protocol of this scale, though it only applies to non-fund-critical parameters.
steelman.green: The core fund-holding contracts are immutable. Governance has no path to upgrade implementations or drain user funds, restricting its blast radius to T2/T3.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the fund-holding core contracts (PoolManager and PositionManager) are immutable, making T1 fund-critical actions unreachable. The governance surface is restricted to T2 (economic parameters) and T3 (internal rules), and is protected by a multi-stage on-chain voting process with a ~10-day execution delay.

evidence.shows: PoolManager owner is the Timelock address 0x1a9C8182C09F50C8318d769245beA52c32BE35BC.
GovernorBravo voting period is 40,320 blocks (~6 days).
